title: Lightly Smoky Russian Pepper Pilaf
description: A savory and pillowy Russian dish featuring roasted peppers and a hint of smokiness from binchotan charcoal, made paleo-friendly with cauliflower rice.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toss the cauliflower florets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, roast the bell peppers over an open flame or in the oven until the skin is charred.
Peel the skin off the roasted peppers and chop them into small pieces.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the roasted cauliflower, chopped peppers, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Smoke the mixture with binchotan charcoal for 5 minutes, or until lightly smoky.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
